stall_name: Fresh Fruit Juice (Pasir Panjang Food Centre)
reviews: Banana milkshake reg 3nett Very good banana, this was pretty good. No condensed milk tho FYI unlike...
Rockmelon milkshake large 4.5nett The rockmelon is not as sweet as the persimmon, this one is sligh...
Persimmon milkshake 3.5nett small Dear god. Probably the best persimmon used here to produce the sw...

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

reviews.author: Jermyn Eng
reviews.text:
Friendly auntie which sells very good combination fruit juice. She is passionate in her creations, and various drinks have specific instructions to follow to attain the best taste from it. First time I get instructions on how to drink fruit juice haha.

But it really tastes great, with full flavour. And they do not believe in adding sugar syrup! Ordered sour plum and guava and it packs a punch!

reviews.author: Keify Toh
reviews.text:
I simply love their Avocado 🥑  fruit juice.  Using the real stuff.

Lady boss is very friendly and deliver the juice over even though I am sitted far away.  Thank you for the great service!!!

reviews.author: Yizhou Wu
reviews.text: Idk if it's just me but I got this persimmon shake and it wasn't as good as before. Less intense for some reason, although apparently the same people are running the place?

reviews.author: Planet Mars Exploration 2033
reviews.text: The drinks is reasonable price. Water melon $2 medium and banana milk shake $3. Hope the rental from NeA continue to maintain low to have affordable food.

reviews.author: Hwee Shan Chia
reviews.text: Delicious drinks with a great range. I love the guava sour plum juice!

reviews: For Cheap and Good Lontong. Westies, come by Pasir Panjang Food Centre for an affordable solution to...
Cheap and Good Nasi Padang & Lontong! Al Ehsan’s lontong - Garnished with dried shrimp chilli, the...

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, #01-01 Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

reviews.author: Rick L
reviews.text:
Missed this place for more than 2 years. Served one of the best Beef Rendang and Fried Chicken at reasonable price

But alas, one look at the stall, the food arrangement seems different, the ppl manning also diff, their fried chicken is smaller than usual n beef rendang taste totally diff too. Apparently they had changed owner but retaining the stall name.

reviews.author: Talvin Singh
reviews.text: Old school nasi padang, one of the very best in Singapore ...

reviews.author: Magdalene Ng
reviews.text: This malay stall runs by 2 ladies has one of the tastiest nasi padang that I've eaten. Even though the variety of dishes are not that many, whatever they sells taste delicious n looks appetizing. Whatever I'm in the area I make sure I'll join the long hungry lunch queue to buy their food. I noticed a lot of the lunch crowd love to buy the fried chicken but for me I love the fried fish and lots of veggies. Moreover its value for your money!

reviews.author: Dylan Ong
reviews.text: Good Nasi Padang served. Has been having for 27 years. 👍😋

reviews.author: Raz Yaacub
reviews.text:
Tried the lontong and nasi lemak. Excellent excellent value ... testament to the long queue during lunch time. The former is unique in that its gravy is surprisingly thick. The lontong is sufficiently soft, while the cabbage serving is generous. You'll have to pair it with the fried egg.
For the latter, I think you can't do without the fried chicken. It's more tumeric-based. Reminds me of home cooking.

stall_name: Khao Kha Moo (Pasir Panjang Food Centre)
reviews: Signature pig knuckles 10nett The ala carte portion is not a lot actually, feeds 1 Despite the lig...

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, #01-13 Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

reviews.author: Benito Linus
reviews.text: The stall name says it all. Khao kah moo, which means braised pork trotter rice in Thai. The menu was simple, it’s either pork trotter rice or pork trotter Tom yum soup. First of all, the sauce was really nice. Very rich, very fragrance and it’s not greasy. Meat was extremely tender with the right amount of fat. It was served with all the usual suspects of braised egg, greens and preserved veggies. Oh, there was also a bowl of soup and it was surprisingly tasty too! Yum! And everything for only $5!

reviews.author: Elaine Chia
reviews.text:
Very nice pig trotters rice. The fat and lean meat proportion was just right. The eggs were very yummy! Goes very well with the rice. The preserved veg goes well with the rice too.

Will definitely want to eat this again.

reviews.author: Yun Ying WONG
reviews.text:
Hands down 1 of the best pig trotters i've had in SG. Everything is flavourful down to the bone. I usually get the pig trotters rice set (#1 on the menu) together with a side of pig trotters by itself (#4 on menu, in chinese it's translated as pig hands so i reckon it's the front legs??)

The boss is a really nice thai middle aged uncle who's super polite and patient, which makes me even happier to patronise his stall. He's closed on sundays, please take note.

It's at Pasir Panjang food centre, just a few minutes' walk from Pasir Panjang station. The place seems generally pretty old but there're plenty of seats to go around (at least for me when i went there at saturday lunchtime), but i bought takeaway instead.

reviews.author: Ding Bang Loo
reviews.text:
As good as it gets, I think it’s rather authentic and the chilli is really good! Portion is big for just $5 and comes with soup, will definitely be back for more!
Uncle is also very friendly and nice 😊

reviews.author: Ray Ng
reviews.text: Tried this Tom Yum Pig Trotters Clear  soup for the first time. Came with Rice. Love its so much. Not too sour or spicy. Strongly recommend that you guys try. I definitely will go back for this dish again.

reviews: Pork Cutlet Carbonara (SGD6.50) Chanced upon this hidden gem at Pasir Panjang Food Centre and decided to give it a try. The carbonara was creamy and the pork cutlet was crispy. Definitely worth the price.

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, #01-41 Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

reviews.author: Flo G
reviews.text:
Good hawker centre right next to MRT station with plenty of good choices at reasonable prices.

There’s a segregation of Malay Indian stalls and a non-halal local side.

Favourites are Jubilant chicken rice!
Many other stalls like roasts, BCM fishball noodles are highly rated.

Also a Japanese ramen with high ratings!

Good coffee too!

I found prices are generally lower here than other hawkers in the city.

Only gripe is the amount of smokers who stood or sit around the hawker centre to smoke. Sometimes the smoke comes right to the seated eating areas.

reviews.author: Geraldine Koh
reviews.text: A spacious spot with plenty of seating, though it can get a little hot during midday. The curry rice is tasty and satisfying — definitely worth trying! The friendly aunty at the stall makes the experience even better, and she accepts all sorts of payment methods, making it super convenient.

reviews.author: Wang Teck Heng
reviews.text: While this place at first look may not seem to have much to offer, it does have a few GEMs. I like the teochew porridge, curry rice, mutton soup, bbq and western here. You just need to try the stalls out and I am sure you will find something to like.

reviews.author: iamjess
reviews.text: Had dinner here, food and service were good, but it was a bit too crowded for a Friday night.

reviews.author: Faye Cheang
reviews.text: Went to this food centre on Wednesday after for early dinner around 5pm. Not many stalls opened. We purposely came for satay and bbq fish. Luckily they opened but we waited 1 hour for the satay. Food was ok. We tried char Kuey Teow, it was ok too.

stall_name: Lucky BBQ Seafood (Pasir Panjang Food Centre)
reviews: Superb Sotong. Order the stingray from Alan and the sotong from Lucky! Lucky's sotong is huge in te...
Boiled Shellfish. Vanessa S. Level 10 Burppler · 3920 Reviews. May 29, 2013 · Family Meals

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, #01-04 Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

reviews.author: Vivien S
reviews.text: As with the other reviews, don’t order from here. Awful food and services. All of the food was either over or undercooked. We paid with a $100-bill UPFRONT, and got the corresponding amount of change. Then when all of the food arrived, the very same lady who took our order asked for our payment again. And kept on coming back to us saying we didn’t pay when we showed her the change we got from her. I do not understand why she can forget she got a $100 bill because it’s very memorable to receive one. It’s also not our fault that the bill ‘disappeared’ from her money pouch. At the end, it’s probably not pursued coz she and her colleagues checked their CCTV or something to verify that we DID make payment. Really, please be careful when ordering from here. Better yet, never order from here.

reviews.author: SS Tan
reviews.text:
It a popular stall and have to wait during peak hours.
The BBQ stingray is a hit and miss with sometimes not fresh or dry
The boneless fried chicken is good but the tofu is just average.
Noodle not bad

reviews.author: Congo
reviews.text: Expensive and worst food in Singapore!!! Ruin the reputation as the food capital in the region. I asked the owner why the food looks so different from the picture in front of the stall. He just said "ya.. the picture is a bit different". The Portion is small and taste like overnight leftovers.. Toufu and chicken pieces are not fresh at all. It is worst than school canteen food. I even found a egg-shell in the veg dish!!!    Price:   Veg-$8, Chicken-$10, Tofu-$10, Rice-$2. Totally paid $40.

reviews.author: Eileen Tay
reviews.text: Waited almost 40 mins for 3 dishes, with the dishes served one after another after a long time. Rice was dry. Service was bad and we were just told to wait despite checking in, only to see other tables being served and the staff was going to talk to other stall holders. Last dish (hot plate tofu) that was served was placed on our table roughly after we asked if we could cancel after waiting for so long. Not recommended.

reviews.author: Bluerie Lin
reviews.text: We ordered the large stingray which was $25. The stingray was quite big and juicy and the sambal chilli was spicy but the paste was a bit too thick. Was looking forward to the onions (cincalok) to match it with the stingray but it was bland and there was no lime was given for the stingray.

stall_name: Fatty Ipoh Noodles (Pasir Panjang Food Centre)
reviews: Wet Fried kway teow. Pretty good, the gravy was flavourful, the texture of the kway teow was on the...
Very good wok hei with excellent flavours. Superb noodles with powerpac flavours with good amount of...
Yummy Yuan Yang Hor Fun. If you're looking to try some yuan yang horfun aka horfun with crispy horfu...
Moonlight Hor Fun (SGD5). Luckily chanced upon this hawker stall when I was looking for dinner near...
Malaysian Zi-Char Style Dishes With Chunky Pork Lard in Pasir Panjang. The Wat Dan Hor with Crispy H...
dishes: Wat Dan Hor with Crispy Hor Fun

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, #01-10 Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

google.google_name: Fatty Ipoh Noodles (肥仔怡保炒粉)

reviews.author: Worgan Freeman
reviews.text:
Best beef horfun ever. Broth has so much egg and flavor I downed most of it before even eating anything else.
Vegetables not soggy. Beef is still tender. Noodles are chewy and not too big. Icing on the cake is the chilli sauce being a little sour and spicy. Honestly everything in the dish was 100%.

reviews.author: Tan Sindy
reviews.text:
Waited 30min for this crispy hor fun, the crispies are deep fried thin pork fats.
Fish in hor fun are fresh, half cooked egg making the whole bowl of hor fun slurring smooth~
The healthy part is it doesn’t taste heavily MSG. Mouth not drying or thirsty after the meal.

reviews.author: lin laung Lwin
reviews.text:
$6.50 for beef hor fun. Right amount of thorough wok hei. Decent portion. Thin slice beef is well marinated with soft texture. Slightly thicker one has a bit gamey taste.
Value for $ ⭐️⭐️⭐️
Taste ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Service ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

reviews.author: Yizhou Wu
reviews.text: It's decent, the texture of the horfun is too soft, the chili is good and works well. Pork lard flavour was good, but not crispy at all

reviews.author: Elizabeth goh Goh
reviews.text: What a gem! Its probably the best horfun I had in SG! Both were good, but I preferred the moonlight horfun over the white one because it was more flavorful. The chilli complimented the savory horfun v well too! Be expected to wait though and if you drive, parking is limited.

stall_name: Alan Banana Leaves BBQ Seafood (Pasir Panjang Food Centre)
cuisine_type: Good For Groups, Seafood, Hawker/Kopitiam, BBQ
reviews: Super Shiok Stingray. Alan's sambal stingray was way better than the one we had at the Chomp Chomp...
Sambal Stingray 12nett(S). Really meaty stingray with a wonderful bite. Their sambal is unique...
Sambal Sotong. Fresh and juicy sotong cooked just nice. Sambal is on the mild side.
Sambal Stingray. Fresh stingray, sambal is the mild type. Good for friends gathering.
One of the better ones! Had tried many seafood stalls in Pasir Panjang Food Ctr but...
Bbq Stingray

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, #01-23/24 Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

google.google_name: Alan Banana Leaves BBQ Seafood Stall 23/24

reviews.author: L. c.
reviews.text:
Just order the stingray. It’s not bad for taste and size at $16.

Experience marred by constant upselling. From rice to fried rice and upsizing the stingray. Rice is also hard.

They will serve to your table upon ordering. Quite fast. Just that the utensils are disposable.

reviews.author: David POOPy (MightyPooPSTEAM)
reviews.text: Portions were reasonable and big. Sambal kangkong was decent. The bbq stingray was not cooked long enough at a proper temperature. Sambal was still very wet and runny. There was no bbq char on the skin and bones. More like a baked stingray than bbq. Very disappointed. First time in my life eating an undercooked bbq stingray. Won't be returning again to this stall.

reviews.author: Merryheart
reviews.text: This stall situated at the Pasir Panjang Food Centre serves good BBQ seafood.  My favourite dishes are sambal stingray & squid which are tender & juicy. The boiled cockles are usually fresh, good to be taken without any dipping. Another item I recommend is the mee goreng (Malay style fried noodles).  The service here is very efficient.  We got our food served within 10 minutes on a busy Saturday night.

reviews.author: Wei Leong
reviews.text:
To be honest the food is so so but I will just share this..I ordered a large stingray amongst other dishes and what was delivered looks acceptable in terms of size...Was eating halfway when the stall assistant came over with some stingray and told me that what I was served is the small and he is topping it up to a large which was what I ordered originally...I mean they could have chose to keep quiet but they didn't so I think that is pretty honest of them to own up.

If u ask me....I wouldn't travel all the way to patronize them but if I am in the area,yes I would.

The egg fried rice is nice.

reviews.author: Teresa Lam
reviews.text: Ordered Sambal Stingray , Sambal Sotong and fried rice . The sambal stingray portion was decent and the stingray was fresh however seasoning was mediocre . the sambal sotong was flavourful and packed with flavours , would recommend this !

stall_name: Ivy's Hainanese Herbal Mutton Soup (Pasir Panjang Food Centre)
reviews: Mutton Soup And Sup Kambing (SGD $17) @ Ivy's Hainanese Herbal Mutton Soup. Established sometime in...
Mutton Soup ($12) The intensity of this soup was mindblowingly good 🤯🤯 The depth in flavour was t...
Mutton Meat Soup 12nett Chock full of ingredients, so I don't think it's worth adding meat cos it's...
Best Mutton Soup! I tried the Herbal Mutton Soup with Mee Sua and an egg $11. The broth quality was...
Herbal Mutton Soup $10 The soup was super tasty, with tender, fatty pieces of meat. Came with piece...
dishes: Herbal Mutton Soup with Mee Sua

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, #01-14 Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

reviews.author: Ivan Teh
reviews.text:
Mutton Soup And Sup Kambing (SGD $17) @ Ivy's Hainanese Herbal Mutton Soup.
.
Established sometime in the year 2000 by Max Ng and named after his wife, this beloved stall continually draws long queues, and on occasion, sells out within 1 hour of opening for the day.
.
They source mutton from New Zealand, and are known to omit ginger in their hot soup / broth served in bubbling clay pots, which is stewed with about 20 different herbs including nam yue / red fermented bean curd paste. Their thin and light soup / broth has deep meaty savoury sweet bitter herbal flavour.
.
The tender boneless mutton meat has meaty savoury flavour, the succulent bone-in mutton ribs slide easily off the bone, the juicy goji berries / wolfberries lend herbal sweet bitter flavour, the crunchy black fungus / wood ear fungus has vegetal earthy savoury flavour, the bouncy button mushrooms carry vegetal earthy sweet flavour, the spongy tofu puffs have grainy savoury flavour, and the crisp fried bean curd sheets have grainy savoury salty flavour. Pairs so well with white rice.

reviews.author: KT
reviews.text: We were lucky going on Wed and it closes at 7.30pm. We arrived at 6.15pm and everything was sold out except meat. Ordered the $14 small portion. The soup was so flavourful and didn't have the mutton smell that puts you off. I drank finished the soup. The meat was tender and generous. Everything was quality and to perfection. Really hard to find nice mutton soup and this did not disappoint. 10/10.

reviews.author: Jonathan Ang
reviews.text: This shop has an almost cult like following and consider me a convert. Strong, pungent, herbal and decadent. Soup wakes you right up with it's herbal and meaty aroma, then suddenly sweet when the goji berries burst. It's on the pricier end but if you're going to have something as rich as this you want the good stuff and they serve the good stuff. Very good value as a heavy meal. Uncle serving sometimes get a little sassy but consider it part of the experience

reviews.author: OB1
reviews.text:
If you are a lamb soup lover, you must make the trip to Ivy Hainanese Herbal Mutton Soup in Pasir Panjang. This place serves what is, in my opinion, the best Hainanese lamb soup I have ever tasted—a true hidden gem.

What sets this soup apart is the broth quality. It's incredibly flavourful, deeply herbal, and tastes rich without being greasy. The lamb itself is tender and fresh, with absolutely none of the gamey smell that sometimes plagues mutton dishes.

The real proof of quality? I didn't feel thirsty at all after finishing the bowl! This confirms that the broth is genuinely slow-boiled and relies on natural ingredients and herbs, not cheap MSG, for its depth of flavour.

Beyond the taste, the value for money is superb. For the quality and generous portion size, the pricing here is incredibly reasonable, especially for Singapore. It feels like you're getting a premium, home-cooked meal for a hawker price.

This is a mandatory visit for anyone who appreciates authentic, nourishing, and expertly prepared lamb soup!

reviews.author: Ida Einjaw
reviews.text: Ivy's hainanese herbal mutton soup. My favourite mutton soup of all time. Not gamey at all and not overcooked, every piece melts in the mouth. Served in a Chinese clay pot. Choices include mix of mutton meat, shanks, leg meat or tendons. Prices start from $12 for their small portion. But it's a filling portion and worth every cent, rich flavours of their secret blend of Chinese herbs, goji berries, tau pok, and beancurd sheets and black fungus mushrooms and spring onions. Served with their fermented beancurd garlic chilli sauce.

stall_name: Bua Siam Thai Seafood (Pasir Panjang Food Centre)
reviews: Pad Thai. Very crowded food centre as it always attract the people who working around here, even for...
Tomyum Goong 8nett. Though the aromatics are strong and there's a decent bit of heat, the soup was s...
Tom Yum ($8). A must-order at Thai restaurants, I found the tom yum here addictive even though it wa...
Basil Pork Rice

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, #01-36 Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

reviews.author: ReversedPi (JacxT)
reviews.text:
First time dining at the food centre, been packing the basil pork rice for take out and decided to dine in today.
It is one of those more authentic thai stalls in food court/hawker setting, i.e. basil pork is supposed to be fried till dry, and this place does it that way (not saucy).
We tried the prawn tanghoon ($18), came with 5 shell-in prawns and flavourful tanghoon fried in coriander roots and garlic.
Fried chilli seabass ($30), chilli sauce is lest the ones u see in other sweet-sour sauce, but is more caramelised chili, topped with pineapple and onions.

Do note that it is a one-man show stall and waiting time can be slightly long during peak hours.

reviews.author: Sheue Lin Ng
reviews.text: The grilled pork neck was good. Tom yum soup is not fantastic even though it has a  lot of mushrooms inside the soup. The garlic chicken is deep fried and juicy. The portions were generous.

reviews.author: YungMing Lee
reviews.text:
Good portions, and one of the better Basil Pork rice around for $5. Pork was flavourful and fried till the gravy was reduced and pork was browned. I very much prefer this to those with runny gravy.
Service can be slow due to staffing, so check for waiting time when ordering.

reviews.author: Thaddeus Zheng
reviews.text:
Order the Tom yum for $8 and $1 for rice. Quite a number of ingredients. Chef make it more to their own style. Good for a rainy day.

I would recommend the basil pork over the Tom yum.

reviews.author: Psion Star
reviews.text:
Ordered tom yum soup and phad thai. Soup has lots of random ingredients not related to tom yum (eg. Fishballs). Phad thai taste nothing like phad thai. It tastes exactly like char kuey tiao.

If authentic thai food is what you are looking for, this is not the place.

stall_name: 216 Choa Chu Kang BBQ Chicken Wing (Pasir Panjang Food Centre)
reviews: 1.5nett per piece, min 3 pieces. It really is good. Slightly crispy, but very well marinated and it's super juicy too.
Awesome BBQ Wings! I have been popping by the Alexandra area for gym after work recently and took the opportunity to explore Pasir Panjang Food Centre.

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, #01-23 Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

google.google_name: 216 Choa Chu Kang BBQ Chicken Wing

reviews.author: Jane Tan
reviews.text: Wings are okay if you aren't picky. Not the best charcoal grilled ones I've eaten. Chili is ok. Don't expect too high and you'll be fine.

reviews.author: LWF SL Leong
reviews.text: Chicken wings taste ok, the chilli sauce is nice. Price are slightly higher than others. Hawker Centre sell at food court price $$.

reviews.author: Terence Lee
reviews.text: Wow randomly saw this place. I used to eat here really often more than 15 years ago back when they were still at 216 CCK Ave 1. Last time I had it was probably in 2013 before they moved. The wings and chilli is still as good though prices have probably increased lol

reviews.author: Alan Ng
reviews.text: Came here at 9:15pm. Managed to help the uncle clear his inventory. Good and crispy. Definitely my recommended choice in Pasir Panjang food centre

reviews.author: Wang Teck Heng
reviews.text: Love it. Finally one that is not the usual BBQ chicken wing chain. Roasted till really crispy, but does not have the paper like skin. I like the way the skin turns out.

stall_name: Yusoff Haji Jalal Satay (Pasir Panjang Food Centre)
reviews: 3 of each, 0.8nett per skewer, min 10 skewers. Put your name down as soon as they open, they are very popular...
Old School Satay Stall. This old-school satay stall hails from the famous satay club at the Esplanade...

other_info.address: 121 Pasir Panjang Road, #01-21 Pasir Panjang Food Centre, Singapore 118543

reviews.author: L. c.
reviews.text:
Upon ordering, you collect a buzzer and wait for it to beep. I like the marinate which is somewhat sweet. $0.80 is the average price for satay currently.

I ordered the beef and chicken. Moist and compact. The satay gravy is not the typical peanut kind. It’s red and complements the meat well.

reviews.author: Talvin Singh
reviews.text:
This stall has its roots at the old satay club (old esplanade) and I have been patronizing them for decades ...

They have their own uniqueness in their satay which is glazed differently as compared to other satays in Singapore ... give it a try, you won't regret it

reviews.author: loneoceans
reviews.text: Classic stall of Pasir Panjang Food Centre. Kind and friendly staff, tasty satays with just the right amount of char. Peanut sauce is a little different from the usual fare. Prices have increased a bit (now 80-90c/pc as of Nov 2023), but the quality and consistency over the years has consolidated its reputation. Recommended!

reviews.author: Jody Chan
reviews.text: The satay is delicious, just the right amount of char.  The dipping sauce is good. Very different from the usual peanut sauce.  It tastes like BBQ

reviews.author: Andy Quek
reviews.text: The worst satay ever. Regret trying this. Gravy was SUPER SUPER OILY. More oil than kuah. Came hom next day had indigestion, vomited and diarrhoea which was full of oil. Pls eat at your own risk.
